title: Ethereum Gas Fee Calculator 2026 | Hacé Cuentas
h1: Calculate Your Ethereum Gas Fees Instantly
description: Calculate Ethereum gas fees instantly. Find the cost of your ETH mainnet transactions, swaps, and smart contracts.
intro: Ethereum gas fees follow blockchain principles: each transaction consumes computational units priced in Gwei. Understand your transaction costs instantly. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed periodically to keep you informed.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **gas units × gwei price × ETH price in USD**. Direct calculation of your Ethereum transaction cost.
useCases: Technical calculations for Ethereum gas fees
Quick reference for smart contract projects
explanation:
**Ethereum transactions** use computational units called gas, priced in Gwei. This calculator applies the standard industry formula to calculate your transaction costs instantly.

## How it's calculated

Gas fee = (gas units × gas price in Gwei) × current ETH price in USD

This calculator applies the formula with your input values, checking typical ranges and alerting you to unusual values.

## Typical transaction costs by operation

| Operation | Gas Units | Cost at 30 Gwei | Example |
|---|---|---|---|
| Simple transfer | 21,000 | ~$2.20 | Send ETH to wallet |
| Token swap | 150,000–200,000 | ~$18–$26 | Uniswap trade |
| NFT mint | 200,000–500,000 | ~$26–$65 | Deploy contract |
| Smart contract interaction | Varies | Varies | Complex operations |

## Gas optimization strategies

- **Use Layer 2 networks**: Arbitrum and Optimism cost ~100× less than Ethereum mainnet.
- **Batch transactions**: Combine multiple actions into a single transaction to share base fees.
- **Time your transactions**: Transact during low-traffic windows (nights/weekends UTC) to avoid peak pricing.
- **Set gas limits carefully**: Too high wastes funds; too low causes transaction failures.
- **Monitor gas tracker tools**: Check real-time gas prices before submitting large transactions.

## Final notes

This calculation is for reference only. For critical financial decisions, consult a qualified professional. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed periodically.

faq.a: Gas is the computational unit measuring the work required to execute transactions on Ethereum. Every operation—transfers, swaps, smart contracts—consumes a specific amount of gas.

faq.q: How do you calculate Ethereum gas fees?
faq.a: Multiply gas units × gas price in Gwei × current ETH price. Example: 21,000 gas × 30 Gwei × $3,500 ETH ≈ $2.20

faq.q: How much does a simple ETH transfer cost?
faq.a: A basic transfer uses 21,000 gas. At 30 Gwei and $3,500 ETH, expect around $2.20 in fees.

faq.q: How much gas do Uniswap swaps use?
faq.a: Token swaps on Uniswap typically consume 150,000–200,000 gas, depending on slippage tolerance and network conditions.

faq.q: Why are gas fees high during certain times?
faq.a: During peak hours (US business hours on weekdays), more transactions compete for block space, pushing gas prices higher. Weekends and nights typically have lower fees.

faq.q: How much gas does NFT minting cost?
faq.a: NFT minting ranges from 200,000–500,000 gas depending on smart contract complexity and metadata requirements.
Yes. Arbitrum and Optimism cost approximately 100× less than Ethereum mainnet because they batch transactions off-chain and settle periodically.

faq.q: How can I reduce my Ethereum gas fees?
faq.a: Use Layer 2 networks, transact during low-traffic hours, batch multiple transactions together, or use gas optimization tools.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Formula is static; does not require external data updates
